title: Hyperthyroidism follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

medication.text: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.
medication.rationale: The exact information and warning-symptom section supports patient questions and safety-net documentation only when discussed by the clinician.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports shared decision documentation, thyroid-medicine interaction information, and specific thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms including shortness of breath, rapid growth, hoarse voice, and swallowing difficulty.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.6 — information and thyroid-enlargement warning symptoms

referral.text: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.
referral.rationale: The exact hyperthyroidism monitoring section supports interval change, symptoms, clinician-reviewed thyroid results, specialist correspondence, entered plan and arranged follow-up without changing medicine or inferring control.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed TSH, FT4, and FT3 monitoring, treatment-context review, and clinician-led follow-up or specialist advice without mapping medicine doses or autonomous treatment.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.7.1–1.8.5 — follow-up and monitoring of hyperthyroidism

source_limitations: Cause, symptoms, thyroid examination, eye disease, rhythm, exact results, medicine, adherence, adverse effects, pregnancy, interpretation and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
